title: REVOLUTION 2026 SPRING MEN'S LEAGUE | WEEK 9 RECAP
content: The regular season is over, and Week 9 went out with a statement. Orange jumped out to a 28-point halftime lead and Red could never dig out, Manny Robertson took over for a Gray squad that needed someone to step up and delivered 31 and 14 to come from behind against Tan, Pink held off Purple by four in a back-and-forth finish that came down to the last minute, White handled Yellow without Donnie Dwyer thanks to a dominant John Helsel performance, and Asko Mostarlic came back and reminded everyone what Green looks like at full strength. Let's get into the action.

games.text: Orange didn't wait to be invited. Red opened with nothing to lose, but Orange came out with something to prove. Trent Roleau set the tone early, attacking the basket and knocking down four threes on his way to a complete first half. Walker Storey was a dual threat all morning, hitting from deep and cleaning up the glass. Will Oboh was the engine of the offense, running pick-and-roll actions and finishing with 7 assists. By the time the half was done, Orange had built an advantage no team was coming back from. Halftime: Orange 51, Red 23. Red found themselves down 28 with a half to play. They responded, but it wasn't enough. Jibril Abdullahi went to work in the second half and put together one of the more remarkable individual efforts of the season to keep it respectable. He finished with 27 points on 11-of-25 shooting with 11 rebounds and 7 assists. Will Ewald added 15 points and 10 rebounds, and Sub Kiley chipped in 13 off the bench. Red actually outscored Orange 40-24 in the second half, but the 28-point halftime hole was already dug. Roleau closed with 24 points and 13 rebounds. Orange cruised. Orange won, 75-63.

performers.Orange: Trent Roleau — 24 pts, 13 REB, 1 AST; Walker Storey — 17 pts, 10 REB, 2 AST; Will Oboh — 10 pts, 5 REB, 7 AST
performers.Red: Jibril Abdullahi — 27 pts, 11 REB, 7 AST; Will Ewald — 15 pts, 10 REB, 2 AST; Sub Kiley — 13 pts, 6 REB

games.text: Manny Robertson was not going to let this game slip away. Tan came out sharp and controlled the first half. Brody Snow was efficient everywhere on the floor, scoring inside and out and hauling in 11 rebounds. Devin Bradford gave Tan a second reliable option and kept the pressure on. Sub Will sparked Tan off the bench with energy and perimeter shooting. For Gray, Kevin Garrison and Kyle DeCoster — the team's two leading scorers all season — weren't in attendance, but Robertson stayed patient and kept Gray within striking distance. Halftime: Tan 38, Gray 31. Gray came out of the half looking like a different team, and Robertson made sure of it. He took over the second half entirely, finishing with 31 points on 11-of-25 shooting and knocking down 7 threes to go with 14 rebounds and 4 assists. Ethan Calle gave Gray a reliable second option with 16 points and 10 rebounds. Dylan Pratico added 12 points and 11 rebounds to give Gray three double-digit scorers without their usual stars. Tan's Snow finished with 23, Bradford added 18, and Sub Will contributed 12, but Gray outscored them 41-26 in the second half and finished the job. Gray won, 72-64.

performers.Gray: Manny Robertson — 31 pts, 14 REB, 4 AST; Ethan Calle — 16 pts, 10 REB, 2 AST; Dylan Pratico — 12 pts, 11 REB, 4 AST
performers.Tan: Brody Snow — 23 pts, 11 REB, 3 AST; Devin Bradford — 18 pts, 9 REB, 1 AST; Sub Will — 12 pts, 5 REB, 4 AST

games.text: Tyrone Conley was on a different level, and Pink let him take over. Pink grabbed control in the first half and never let go. Conley attacked with purpose, getting to the free-throw line repeatedly and finishing a perfect 11-of-12 from the stripe on his way to a first-half performance that had Purple scrambling. Kyle Haley was equally impressive, connecting on threes at a remarkable clip. For Purple, Kaleb Swetty knocked down three first-half threes and kept the game within reach. Trevis Buckhannon was a wall on the boards, and Jimmy Warden contributed points, rebounds, and playmaking on both ends to keep Purple in the fight. Halftime: Pink 41, Purple 34. Purple rallied in the second half, but Pink made just enough plays to hold on. Swetty was the story of the second half for Purple, finishing with 27 points on 10-of-19 shooting and 6-of-11 from three — one of his best offensive outputs of the season. Buckhannon finished with 19 rebounds, one of the most dominant rebounding performances of the year. But Conley would not let this one get away. He closed with 32 points on 11-of-12 from the line and 11 rebounds. Haley finished with 29 points on 7 threes. Pink held on by four to end the regular season with a win. Pink won, 80-76.

performers.Pink: Tyrone Conley — 32 pts, 11 REB, 3 AST; Kyle Haley — 29 pts, 6 REB, 1 AST; Edo Semic — 11 pts, 5 REB, 1 AST
performers.Purple: Kaleb Swetty — 27 pts, 1 REB, 3 AST; Brandon Huestis — 17 pts, 4 REB; Jimmy Warden — 12 pts, 6 REB, 3 AST

games.text: Donnie Dwyer was out, and John Helsel made sure no one noticed. White built a commanding first-half lead behind a Helsel performance that was impossible to contain. He went straight at Yellow's interior defense, drawing fouls and converting at the line on 8-of-9 attempts. Zaire Wilson was a force on both ends, scoring and rebounding on every possession, while Sub Tavarius gave White a significant offensive punch off the bench. Yellow battled throughout, with Drew Pelkey scoring and distributing in equal measure, but White's first-half cushion was too steep. Halftime: White 49, Yellow 35. Yellow mounted a second-half run that made White earn every basket down the stretch. Pelkey finished with 22 points, 9 rebounds, and 5 assists in another complete performance. Logan Letourneau contributed 16 points and 5 assists. Cam Perta knocked down four threes and scored 12. Yellow outscored White 41-34 in the second half, but the 14-point halftime deficit was too much to overcome. Helsel closed with 28 points and 14 rebounds, and Wilson added 25 points and 15 rebounds. White finishes the regular season with one of the strongest cases in the West and heads into the playoffs with momentum. White won, 83-76.

performers.White: John Helsel — 28 pts, 14 REB; Zaire Wilson — 25 pts, 15 REB, 4 AST; Sub Tavarius — 18 pts, 6 REB, 1 AST
performers.Yellow: Drew Pelkey — 22 pts, 9 REB, 5 AST; Logan Letourneau — 16 pts, 5 REB, 5 AST; Cam Perta — 12 pts, 2 REB

games.text: Asko Mostarlic is back, and the league has been warned. Green's last game of the regular season was a statement from start to finish. With Mostarlic back in the lineup, the offense operated on a different level. He was the first option, the best option, and the most efficient option, shooting 6-of-13 from three and getting to his spots with ease. Mahlik Franklin was equally dominant, taking over the paint and running the break. Andy Bousono did everything else — directing traffic, hitting threes, and finishing just a rebound shy of a triple-double. By the time the half was over, it was a formality. Halftime: Green 58, Blue 36. Green won the second half, too. Mostarlic finished with 33 points on 13-of-21 shooting. Franklin closed with 29 points, 15 rebounds, and 8 assists in one of the most complete all-around lines of the season. Bousono finished with 16 points, 9 rebounds, and 10 assists. For Blue, Joe Ruggles hit 6 threes and scored 21 in a gutsy individual effort. Wyatt Jarvis-Comi added 15 points and 10 rebounds. Nick Lynn chipped in 15 more. But Green's execution from tip to buzzer was flawless. The regular season ends at Green's terms. Green won, 111-75.

performers.Green: Asko Mostarlic — 33 pts, 8 REB, 4 AST; Mahlik Franklin — 29 pts, 15 REB, 8 AST; Andy Bousono — 16 pts, 9 REB, 10 AST
performers.Blue: Joe Ruggles — 21 pts, 7 REB, 1 AST; Nick Lynn — 15 pts, 9 REB; Wyatt Jarvis-Comi — 15 pts, 10 REB, 1 AST

sponsors.name: Alta Group & The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub
sponsors.message: A huge thank you to the Alta Group and The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub for their continued support of the Revolution Men's League. None of this happens without you.

sponsors.name: Warden's Wisdom
sponsors.message: This recap is brought to you by Warden's Wisdom — a mindfulness-based coaching business dedicated to helping individuals relieve anxiety through personalized mindfulness practices. The regular season is over. What comes next will require your best.
sponsors.quote: The regular season tests your consistency. The playoffs test your character.

closing: Nine weeks of Revolution basketball are in the books. Playoff basketball is next. Tune in starting Saturday at 8 am, and be sure to follow the action into Sunday, starting at 10 am.
